pattern: With two or more movable front letters you can realize any permutation, so the answer is simply the sorted string; with only one you are limited to rotations, so the answer is the smallest rotation.
hints: Consider the two regimes separately: what can you achieve moving only the very first letter, versus moving any of the first few.
When you may pick among at least two front letters, repeated moves let you bubble letters past one another, so every arrangement is reachable.
If k is one, take the lexicographically smallest of all n rotations; otherwise the best reachable string is the characters sorted ascending.

edge_inputs.description: k of one only allows rotations
k of three permits full sorting
k equals two already permits sorting
best rotation is not the trivial one
k equal to length sorts everything

stress.description: random lowercase strings with k forced to one to exercise rotation search
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, 1]
stress.note: small alphabet makes tied rotations common

stress.description: random strings with k at least two so sorting applies
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefgh') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(2, n)
    return [s, k]

complexity.time: O(n^2) for k=1, O(n log n) otherwise
